findings.description: The welcome slide's two-panel layout (left panel with audio troubleshooting, right panel with meeting title/date) is collapsed into a single column in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a clear two-column layout: left side has audio troubleshooting on light blue background, right side has 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting' on cork-texture background. HTML renders these as stacked single-column content.

findings.description: The decorative cork-texture background on the right panel of the welcome slide is missing, losing the visual distinction of the meeting title area.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a cork/bulletin-board textured background behind the meeting title and date. HTML shows plain white background.

findings.description: The green highlight box around 'Say Hi in the chat box...' and 'Please mute' text is missing in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows green/mint colored box highlighting the chat instruction text. HTML renders as plain text without visual emphasis box.

findings.description: The strip of wildlife/habitat images along the right side of the Coastal Habitat Protection Plan Timeline slide are displaced from their original position adjacent to the table.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a vertical strip of nature images (egret, oysters, pufferfish, sea turtle, coral reef fish) alongside the right edge of the timeline table. HTML shows these images stacked above the table, separated from their context.

findings.description: Audio Troubleshooting section is presented as a standalone h2 heading, but in the PDF it is part of the first slide's left panel, not a separate document section. More importantly, it appears AFTER the h1 title and an image, disrupting the logical reading order compared to the PDF where it was a side panel.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, 'AUDIO TROUBLESHOOTING' is a panel on the left side of slide 1. In the HTML, it appears as an h2 after the main title and an image, making it look like a major section of the document rather than supplementary instructions.

findings.description: The agenda content appears twice - once as a text listing on page 2 of the PDF and once as a table on the same page. In the PDF, pages 2 shows the same agenda in two formats (text list and structured layout). The HTML only has the table version, but the agenda text from the first page's icebreaker text is also repeated in the body after the audio troubleshooting section.
findings.evidence: The text 'Say "Hi" in the chat box, let us know who you work for, and tell us your favorite beach.' and 'Please mute' appear both in the h1 area (from the title slide) and again as standalone paragraphs below the audio troubleshooting section.

findings.description: Alt text for timeline images is somewhat speculative. The PDF shows a vertical strip of nature/wildlife photos on the right side of slide 3, and the alt texts attempt to describe them but may not be fully accurate.
findings.evidence: Alt texts include 'Egret in marsh', 'Fish in reef, scallop in seagrass, and sandbar', 'Grouper in reef' - these are reasonable guesses but combine multiple separate images into fewer img tags with combined descriptions.

findings.description: The page number '3' from the PDF slide 3 is missing in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF slide 3 shows '3' as a page/slide number. This is omitted in the HTML, which is acceptable for page numbers.
